expectations.evidence: Output references coding-rules documents by name: typescript.md, naming.md, code-style.md, folder-structure.md, comments.md, git.md. Each ESLint rule includes a 'Source' column tracing back to the originating document.

expectations.text: Detects framework or identifies Base TypeScript
expectations.evidence: Framework Detection section states: 'No package.json found in the project root. This is a skills/references repository, so Base TypeScript configuration was used.'

expectations.text: Maps coding rules to ESLint rules
expectations.evidence: ESLint section contains a mapping table with 14 rules: @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any from typescript.md, @typescript-eslint/consistent-type-definitions from typescript.md, no-restricted-syntax from code-style.md, simple-import-sort from folder-structure.md, jsdoc/require-jsdoc from comments.md, etc.

eval_feedback.overall: All assertions passed. The without_skill run also produced comprehensive output with ESLint rule mapping and framework detection, comparable to the with_skill run.
